summary: Security review accepts the M6 release-readiness smoke task. The workspace diff stays limited to local smoke coverage, README/.env release-readiness guidance, and project handoff/state; no runtime app, lib, service, agent, component, Prisma, package-lock, dependency, or provider wiring changes were found. README and .env.example use local defaults plus blank future-provider placeholders, warn against committed real secrets, and keep public deployment, production auth/session/ownership policy, paid providers, CI/CD, DNS, account creation, and commercial release decisions human-gated. The focused release smoke is deterministic, blocks global fetch, uses the mock scan path, avoids secrets/network/browser/public deployment, and covers radar task context, persisted opportunity/detail readiness, and MVP Spec generation readiness.

security_checklist.notes: C:\Users\Administrator\.codex\skills\project-git-workflow\scripts\git\Status.cmd showed only .env.example, README.md, project task state, the release handoff, and tests/release-readiness-smoke.test.ts. A protected-path status check over package.json, package-lock.json, agents, services, lib, app, components, prisma, mock-data, docs, .agents, and .codex returned no output. package.json was reviewed and remains unchanged.

security_checklist.item: No real secrets or realistic tokens
security_checklist.notes: The focused secret-pattern scan over README.md, .env.example, the release smoke test, and the release-engineer handoff returned no matches. .env.example keeps future provider/payment/cron/deployment values blank, including OPENAI_API_KEY, SERP_API_KEY, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, CRON_SECRET, and PUBLIC_APP_URL.

.env.example:1 identifies local 48-hour MVP validation, .env.example:10 pins SERP_PROVIDER=mock, .env.example:13 says production auth is not implemented, and .env.example:19 warns to leave future LLM keys blank and not commit real keys. Optional SERP, email, payment, cron, and deployment placeholders remain blank.

security_checklist.item: README local-only validation and secret posture
security_checklist.notes: README.md:24-27 says the default smoke path is mock/deterministic and needs no provider accounts, secrets, network, browser, or public deployment. README.md:52-53 tells developers not to add real secrets to .env.example, README examples, tests, or committed files.

README.md:20 and README.md:116-121 state that production auth/session/ownership policy, public deployment, paid provider setup, CI/CD, DNS, store listings, commercial release decisions, and real account creation remain human-gated and are not executed by this repository setup.

tests/release-readiness-smoke.test.ts:31 blocks global fetch, tests/release-readiness-smoke.test.ts:40 runs useMockSerp=true, tests/release-readiness-smoke.test.ts:83 serializes opportunity detail readiness, tests/release-readiness-smoke.test.ts:97 generates the MVP Spec, and tests/release-readiness-smoke.test.ts:111 asserts zero blocked network attempts.

The release-engineer handoff validates and accurately describes the local/no-deployment scope, no package or runtime changes, no real secrets, and the lead validation evidence supplied in the formal review request.

tests_run.result: Reviewed working tree on main: modified .env.example, README.md, and project task state; untracked release-engineer handoff, security-reviewer handoff, and tests/release-readiness-smoke.test.ts.
Reviewed tracked diff. README/.env changes are documentation and placeholder changes only; task state moved from IN_PROGRESS/release_engineer to REVIEW/security_reviewer.
PASS: 1 focused release-readiness smoke test passed.

tests_run.command: rg secret/token pattern scan over README.md .env.example tests\release-readiness-smoke.test.ts and release-engineer handoff
tests_run.result: PASS: no real-seeming API keys, bearer tokens, Stripe tokens, webhook secrets, password assignments, or nonblank secret placeholders matched.

tests_run.command: git status --short -- package.json package-lock.json agents services lib app components prisma mock-data docs .agents .codex
tests_run.result: PASS: no protected runtime, dependency, package-lock, Prisma, docs/mock-data, agent/service/lib/app/component, .agents, or .codex path changes were present.

tests_run.command: rg fetch/http/provider/env posture scan over tests\release-readiness-smoke.test.ts README.md .env.example
tests_run.result: PASS: hits were expected localhost docs, blank/local env placeholders, README explanatory text, and the smoke test's fetch blocker. No external URL, provider construction, process.env read, or paid-provider wiring was introduced by the smoke test.
PASS: no whitespace errors. Git printed LF-to-CRLF working-copy warnings only.

decisions: ACCEPT: The smoke test is deterministic and local-only, blocks fetch, and exercises existing product boundaries without adding runtime behavior.
ACCEPT: README and .env.example improve secret hygiene by replacing real-looking provider examples with local defaults and blank optional future placeholders.
ACCEPT: README keeps public deployment and production operational decisions explicitly human-gated instead of automating or implying deployment readiness execution.
ACCEPT: The workspace contains no package-lock, dependency, provider wiring, Prisma, runtime app/lib/service/agent/component, CI/CD, DNS, or public deployment changes.
No product files were modified during security review. Only the requested security-reviewer handoff was added.
risks: The manual demo flow uses localhost API calls and assumes a developer has intentionally run the documented local Prisma migrate/seed commands before starting the dev server.
The MVP remains intentionally single-admin/local and unauthenticated; public exposure still requires a later production auth/session/ownership decision.
Future real provider, email, payment, cron, deployment, and CI/CD setup remains out of scope and must be reviewed separately before execution.
The current local Windows build environment may still print nonfatal Next SWC optional-native warnings, as recorded by the release engineer.
requested_action: Proceed with QA review for M6-RELEASE-READINESS-SMOKE-001. Security review accepts the release-readiness smoke and documentation posture with local-MVP residual risks noted and no requested changes.
